title: Root source directories allow docs and package metadata to survive proposer edits
reasoning: When srcDir is configured as ".", isUnderSourceDir returns true for every path, and isAllowedReducePath then accepts any .js/.ts/.jsx/.tsx/.mjs/.cjs file that is not a declaration or test file. changedSource relies on this predicate to decide which modified files count as source. In a monorepo or repo-root source configuration, a reducer can modify build scripts, config files, docs examples, benchmark helpers, or other non-source JavaScript/TypeScript files outside the intended reduction surface, and those edits will be scored as legitimate source changes instead of being rejected as out-of-surface changes. The existing tests cover normal srcDir="src" and node_modules, but not the root-source case for common non-source paths such as package.json-adjacent config files or scripts.
reproduction: Configure CN_SRC_DIR=. and let a reduce proposal edit vitest.config.ts or scripts/release.ts. changedSource will include the path because git diff is scoped to "." and isAllowedReducePath(path, ".") returns true for those extensions, so the candidate can be scored as an allowed reduce edit.
recommendation: For srcDir=".", intersect allowed reduce paths with configured detected regions or explicitly reject known non-source roots and config/build files. Alternatively make region detection produce the authoritative allowlist and have changedSource validate against that allowlist instead of treating every code-like file in the repo as source.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The path-surface tests only assert srcDir="src" behavior and do not exercise srcDir="." with files that are code-like but outside application source.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests for isAllowedReducePath or the higher-level reduce-surface check showing that, with srcDir=".", package/build/test tooling files such as vitest.config.ts, scripts/release.ts, and benchmark helpers are rejected unless they are in a detected source region.
minimumFixScope: Tighten reduce path authorization in orchestrator.ts and update the caller/tests that rely on it.
